title: rubus infracanus
intro: Rubus infracanus, commonly known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a species of raspberry native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients, offering several health benefits, such as improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of certain cancers, and enhanced immune function. Rubus infracanus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to support overall well-being.
